source: our own error map (where we miss, and where we beat the market)

split.line: Over the last 32 graded games, about 42% of our margin miss is model process we can improve — how we set roles, minutes and matchups — and about 58% is shooting luck, which no model controls.
split.bias_line: On average our call sits about 5.5 points off in one direction: we lean toward the home team.

side.line: We picked the right winner in 22 of 32 games (68.8%).

error_map.heading: Where our game calls miss — updated every game

headline_miss.line: Our biggest trustworthy weak spot right now is minutes calls in the in-game injury exit group (games with known disruptions) — off by 13.6 on average across 21 games, about 196% worse than our usual minutes miss of 4.6.

trend.label: Flat so far — the earlier half (14.9 avg miss) and the recent half (12.5) are about the same, so no real trend to report at 21 calls.

legend: Positive edge = we beat the market on average; negative = the market beat us. 'Thin sample' means too few games to trust yet.
